topic: Post-patch c4 review — confirms ISS-1180 "third"->"one of at least three" hedge applied to Pattern paragraph opening sentence, resolves the c3-flagged internal-inconsistency (moderate hole #1). Cross-WIN hedge consistency with WIN-058 and WIN-062 now holds. Pre-existing minor issues (credit-paragraph redundancy, onclick return-false UX) remain unresolved but are carry-forwards, not regressions.
our_argument_summary: WIN-058b’s Pattern paragraph was patched on 2026-04-20 per ISS-1180 to change the opening ‘This is the third acknowledge-without-fix move’ to ‘This is one of at least three acknowledge-without-fix moves.’ The c3 review flagged this opening sentence as internally inconsistent with the closing sentence (‘At least three documentary concessions’) and recommended a matching dual-hedge, aligning WIN-058b with the dual-hedge treatment WIN-058 already received. The patch lands as recommended: fresh wins.json detail_evidence_length grew from 5813 to 5826 (+13 chars), which matches exactly the character delta of ‘This is the third acknowledge-without-fix move’ (44 chars) -> ‘This is one of at least three acknowledge-without-fix moves’ (57 chars) = +13. Opening and closing both now say ‘at least three.’ Cross-WIN consistency check: WIN-058 uses ‘at least three instances in commit 6e06efb’ (heading), ‘one of at least three parallel documentary concessions’ (body), ‘At least three acknowledgements in a single commit’ (closing) — fully hedged. WIN-058b now uses ‘one of at least three acknowledge-without-fix moves’ (opening), ‘At least three documentary concessions in a single 24-hour window’ (closing) — dual hedge present. WIN-062 uses ‘one of at least three parallel acknowledge-without-fix moves’ (single-sentence treatment) — hedge present. All three targets consistent. The verdict (Not Demonstrated), all four code_analysis tags (monitoring=none, relabels_standard=false, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false), and the scientific core (post-hoc ratio, Monte Carlo rebuttal of r=0.992, no repository code computing either the r-value or the 10.9/6.5 ratio) are untouched by this patch and remain defensible. The c3 review’s two minor carry-forward items (credit-paragraph redundancy and onclick return-false UX) were not part of the ISS-1180 patch scope and persist unchanged; they are flagged again here as carry-forward not-regression, severity minor. One new subtle issue surfaces on close read: WIN-058 pins the pattern to ‘single commit’ (6e06efb) while WIN-058b uses the looser ‘single 24-hour window’ framing. This is pre-existing (not introduced by ISS-1180) and defensible under the ‘at least three’ hedge (a fourth concession might live in a different commit), but it is a framing asymmetry a careful reader can spot.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: N/A — prose-only edit inside detail_evidence. No monitor.py / pull_data.py reach. No code_analysis tag changes, no aggregate counts affected, no predictions.json reach. Pure textual hedge inside a single sentence.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A for this cycle. Underlying κ=1.677 remains a hardcoded HTML ratio (established in c2, unchanged); the r=0.992 correlation remains static-in-HTML (no repo code computes it); the 10.9/6.5 ratio is not computed in any script.
data_sources_used: monitor/changes/chg-20260417-1620-001, -004, -005 (referenced via citation chain, not re-fetched)
claim_index.json verbatim quotes — transcribed in detail_evidence, not re-fetched this run
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The ISS-1180 patch preserves the ‘at least’ hedge rather than committing to a definite count. This is defensible (ai_manifest.json registry_semantics is a candidate fourth in the same 2026-04-17 commit window, to be enumerated when ISS-1087 resolves), but without a parenthetical pointer to the candidate fourth, a careful dome defender can still ask ‘at least three — named three — where’s the fourth?’. This was raised in c3 (stronger_arguments[0]); ISS-1180 deliberately opted for the simpler one-phrase hedge rather than the expanded enumerate-fourth version. That’s a judgment call and either version is acceptable; the c3 reviewer explicitly called the deferred enumeration ‘defensible.’
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None introduced by ISS-1180 patch. The patch is surgical: one sentence, +13 chars, exactly matches the recommended edit shape. No code-claim text changed in this cycle.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No API/fetch call for WIN-058b in monitor.py or pull_data.py (verified c2/c3). The κ=1.677 ratio, the r=0.992 correlation, and the Exhibit A statistic are all static HTML on the dome home page — no script computes them. ISS-1180 is a prose edit and does not touch any code pathway. Tag unchanged, consistent with c2/c3.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: A universal magnetic-gravity coupling constant has no direct standard-physics analog. The phenomenon (correlated SG/magnetometer signal during G5 storms) IS known standard physics (shared electromagnetic forcing, ground-induced current artifacts per Crossley et al. 2013), but the dome frames it as a new coupling mechanism rather than relabeling a known one. ISS-1180 does not touch this framing. Tag unchanged, consistent with c2/c3.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The 2003 Halloween-storm SG/magnetometer data was published in the early 2000s; the dome’s κ=1.677 ‘prediction’ is a ratio extracted from that prior data. detail_evidence still acknowledges this directly (‘A ratio extracted from one event is a measurement, not a prediction’). ISS-1180 does not touch this framing. Tag unchanged, consistent with c2/c3.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No equation in the dome model derives κ=1.677 from disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height=9086 km, sun_altitude, moon_altitude, or any other dome-specific parameter. The constant is 10.9/6.5, a ratio of two measured quantities. ISS-1180 does not touch this framing. Tag unchanged, consistent with c2/c3.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward from c3 hole #2 — redundancy in the credit paragraph. After quoting the verbatim registry_integrity_notes text and stating ‘This κ-ratio claim is now keyed win-058-bedrock-kappa (status: pending_independent_verification; claim_class: pending_contested; domain: gravity_magnetic_coupling). The API entry carries its own honest_caveat: "Independent raw-data reproduction or a successful August 12 2026 Tier 3 result is still required."’, the next sentence says ‘…the API no longer conflates the θ-rule with this coupling ratio, and the new claim_key registers this entry as pending_independent_verification from the outset, with an honest caveat that independent reproduction is required. We credit both moves.’ The second clause restates the status+caveat information already presented two sentences earlier. ‘We credit both moves’ then frames this as two distinct concessions, but move 2 (pending-from-the-outset + honest caveat) is really one concession already documented by the two preceding sentences. This is pre-existing from the ISS-1158 patch (c3) and was NOT in scope for ISS-1180. Not regressed, not newly introduced — reflagging only so it is not lost.
holes_found.recommendation: Defer to next edit pass. ISS-1180 focused narrowly on the hedge fix and did not aim to clean up the credit paragraph. When WIN-058b gets its next substantive edit, apply the c3 recommendation: either drop the restatement (‘That is genuine documentary progress — the API no longer conflates the θ-rule with this coupling ratio. We credit the acknowledgement.’) or reframe ‘both moves’ to enumerate the two actual moves (disambiguation + honest_caveat requirement). One-line patch, non-urgent.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward from c3 hole #3 — two onclick=‘showTab(…);return false’ anchors in WIN-058b (WIN-012 reference and Timestamp Error reference) still cancel the default anchor jump while switching the tab. A reader who clicks ‘WIN-012’ lands at the top of the Wins tab rather than at the WIN-012 panel; a reader who clicks ‘Timestamp Error tab’ lands at the top of that tab rather than at id=‘timestamp-error’. A fix was filed as a new ISS-1158 (closed in closed-issues.json) but the WIN-058b instances have not yet been patched. Not regressed, not newly introduced — reflagging only so it is not lost.
holes_found.recommendation: Batch with the credit-paragraph redundancy on next edit pass. Remove ‘return false’ from both onclick handlers in WIN-058b so the default anchor jump runs after the tab switch. Verify destination anchors (id=‘win012’, id=‘timestamp-error’) land at a readable position in the switched-tab DOM.

holes_found.description: Framing asymmetry (pre-existing, not introduced by ISS-1180) between WIN-058 and WIN-058b/WIN-062 on the scope of the ‘at least three’ concession window. WIN-058’s Pattern paragraph pins the pattern to ‘commit 6e06efb (2026-04-17)’ (specific commit) and then says ‘At least three acknowledgements in a single commit.’ WIN-058b says ‘At least three documentary concessions in a single 24-hour window.’ WIN-062 says ‘inside a 24-hour window on 2026-04-17.’ The ‘commit’ vs ‘24-hour window’ scoping is not identical. Per chg-20260417-1620-001 (methodology.json timestamp_scope, commit 6e06efb) and chg-20260417-1620-004 (claim_index.json registry_integrity_notes, commit 6e06efb), both concessions are in the SAME commit; chg-005 references commits 6e06efb, 5021eec, a57e731 for the broader documentation-surface rewrites including the ai_manifest.json registry_semantics candidate-fourth, which IS spread across a multi-commit window. So WIN-058’s ‘single commit’ framing is correct for the three named concessions but excludes the candidate fourth, and WIN-058b/WIN-062’s ‘24-hour window’ framing is correct for the broader ‘at least three’ hedge. Both are defensible; neither is factually wrong. But a careful reader may notice the scope-width difference and ask which is canonical.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional normalization — either (a) tighten WIN-058b/WIN-062 to ‘single commit’ (if the hedge is meant to stop at the three enumerated concessions all in 6e06efb); or (b) loosen WIN-058 to ‘single 24-hour window’ or ‘same commit wave’ (if the hedge is meant to accommodate the candidate fourth in a possibly-different commit). Not urgent — the asymmetry costs credibility only under unusually careful reading. If the dome author were to push back, our response would be ‘three named concessions are in the same commit; the at-least hedge accommodates parallel concessions in adjacent commits in the 24-hour window; both framings are consistent with the evidence.’ That response is clean, so patching the prose is optional.

stronger_arguments.description: Adversarial-Advocate: a dome defender’s strongest available rebuttal to the ‘acknowledge-without-fix’ framing is that for auditors who follow ai_manifest.json’s recommended_read_order (ai_manifest → methodology → review-response → claim_index → wins.html), the registry_integrity_notes ARE a structural fix — the API layer correctly disambiguates win-058-bedrock-kappa from win-058-theta-rule, and a compliant auditor never trips on the visible duplicate. WIN-058b’s Pattern paragraph does address this indirectly (‘Each fix lives exactly where an AI auditor is told to look and nowhere a human reader will see’) but could preempt the defense more cleanly by acknowledging the conditional: ‘if and only if auditors follow recommended_read_order, the API fix suffices; for auditors who do not (including all human readers and any AI reviewer that reads the human-facing surface directly), the collision persists.’ That sharpens the critique from ‘acknowledge-without-fix’ to ‘acknowledge-with-conditional-fix-that-depends-on-auditor-behavior.’ The verdict stands either way, but the sharper framing is more defensible under advocate-mode attack.
sources: WIN-058b detail_evidence ‘Each fix lives exactly where an AI auditor is told to look’
WIN-062 detail_evidence ‘route AI auditors around it via claim_key’ (same framing, already present)

stronger_arguments.description: Carry-forward from c3 stronger_arguments[1] — the post-hoc critique of κ=1.677 would be strengthened by direct citations showing the 10.9 μGal gravity residuals and 6.5 nT magnetic deltas (numerator and denominator of the ratio) were independently published in peer-reviewed 2003 Halloween-storm analyses BEFORE the dome first labeled κ=1.677 as a prediction. This closes the ‘the dome predicted 1.67 before anyone measured it’ counterargument completely. Still useful for Tier 3 August 2026 pre-registration; not affected by ISS-1180.
sources: Halloween 2003 superconducting gravimeter literature (Crossley, Hinderer et al.)
dome git history — PRED-KAPPA-001 first-mention date

kernel_of_truth.description: The ISS-1180 patch is a clean surgical fix for the c3-flagged internal inconsistency. One sentence, +13 chars, matches the recommended edit shape exactly, preserves the ‘at least three’ hedge everywhere in WIN-058b, and aligns WIN-058b with the dual-hedge treatment WIN-058 already had. Decider did the right thing and sized the patch correctly: this was a rhetorical-consistency fix, not a scientific-claim fix, and the patch treated it as such. No scope creep into the unrelated carry-forward minor issues — correct discipline for a narrow ISS.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Credit for the clean patch does not change the underlying verdict. WIN-058b remains Not Demonstrated because (a) κ=1.677 is a post-hoc ratio from one event, (b) no repository code computes either the r-value or the 10.9/6.5 ratio, (c) Monte Carlo shows r=0.99 is the expected shared-forcing-driven correlation at G5 SNR regardless of any dome coupling mechanism, and (d) derives_from_dome=false. The hedge patch is orthogonal to the verdict — it cleans up a rhetorical wrinkle in the Pattern paragraph, not the scientific core.

deeper_analysis: This is a post-patch verification review, narrow scope by design. The ISS-1180 commit message describes the patch as ‘count hedge third->at-least-three applied to pattern paragraph. Ensure consistency with WIN-058/062 is correct.’ I verified both halves. Quantitative Verification: detail_evidence_length=5826 matches the expected c3-fingerprint (5813) + exactly 13 chars for ‘This is the third acknowledge-without-fix move’ (44) -> ‘This is one of at least three acknowledge-without-fix moves’ (57). Both opening and closing sentences in WIN-058b’s Pattern paragraph now use ‘at least three.’ Cross-WIN Consistency: WIN-058 fully hedged (three mentions), WIN-058b now dual-hedged (opening + closing), WIN-062 single-sentence-hedged. All three targets use the same phrase. Advocate Mode: the strongest rebuttal available to a dome defender on this specific patch is ‘you still haven’t named the fourth, so why hedge?’ That defense was already anticipated by c3 stronger_arguments[0] and explicitly deferred pending ISS-1087 resolution; the current ISS-1180 patch is consistent with that deferral. The second-strongest defense is on the underlying ‘acknowledge-without-fix’ characterization, which assumes compliant auditors read human-facing surfaces rather than machine-readable APIs — also pre-existing and addressed indirectly in the detail_evidence, stronger-argument sharpening offered above in stronger_arguments[0]. No new holes introduced by ISS-1180. All three c4 holes flagged above (credit redundancy, onclick UX, framing-width asymmetry) are pre-existing carry-forwards, not regressions. Verdict Not Demonstrated stands. recommended_action=no_change because ISS-1180 landed correctly and there is no c4-specific additional patch required; the three minor carry-forwards were pre-existing and should be batched into the next substantive WIN-058b edit pass rather than driven by this queue item.
